page_content:
LARC221 Digital Design Tools (3 Credits) The development and application of computing skills as used by the landscape architecture profession. This Computer-Aided Design and Drafting (CADD) course develops computer drafting using a variety of software programs. It also introduces students to Geographic Information Systems (GIS) mapping technologies. Prerequisite: LARC120 and LARC141. Recommended: LARC240 and LARC265. Restriction: Sophomore standing or higher; and must be in Landscape Architecture program.

LARC152 Greening Cities: Who Wins, Who Loses, and Who Cares? (3 Credits) "Greening Cities" can have many interpretations: improving or adding urban economic activity, realizing energy efﬁciency, greening urban transport systems, etc. An important component of livable and sustainable cities and metropolitan ecosystems are the plants and landscapes that are inhabited by plants. With the majority of humans now living in cities, a survey of urban ecosystem principles and an examination of design and planning strategies for plant and landscape resources in urban and metropolitan regions is critical. Credit Only Granted for: LARC152 or PLSC289I. Formerly: PLSC289I.
